name: J. C. Heard
abstract: James Charles Heard (August 10, 1917 – September 27, 1988) was an American swing, bop, and blues drummer.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon-Erik Kellso
about_quotes.evidence: I was in this group, J.C. Heard’s Big Band, and of course J.C. Heard was one of the great jazz drummers and he settled in Detroit and had a very good big band that I played with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon-Erik Kellso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Gabriel
about_quotes.evidence: I stayed with J.C. nine years. That’s the longest I stayed with one band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charlie Gabriel interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Then I went on down with J.C. Heard. And that was really a fine job, 'cause J.C. Heard is so marvelous... I went with J.C. Heard... J.C. Heard band was there... J.C. asked me to play one because Budd Johnson had quit the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I was working at Cafe Society with J. C. Heard... Well, it was J. C. Heard, he had the band, and there was Dicky Wells... and Al McKibbon... Let's see, who was -- Bud Johnson... It was a nice little group

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Teddy engaged his favourite drummer J.C. Heard, who had been a member of Teddy's big band... The only ones in this band that were not new to me were... and of course, Teddy.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Locke
about_quotes.evidence: And he came off the stage, and I told him I said “you know what, J.C.?... I said “man when I was in high school,” I said “you know man I got your autograph.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Locke interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, NY, January 13, 2001
